claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n - 2 is divisible by n
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n, n) - 2) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 3^n - 3 is divisible by n
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(3, n, n) - 3) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then n^2 divides 2^n - 2
For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 6 divides 2^n + 3^n - 5
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n, 6) + pow(3, n, 6) - 5) % 6 == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^(n-1) ≡ 1 mod n
For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then n^3 divides 2^{n^2} - 2^n
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n*n, n*n*n) - pow(2, n, n*n*n)) % (n*n*n) == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 3^(n-1) ≡ 1 mod n
For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 6^(n-1) ≡ 1 mod n
For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 5 divides 2^n + 3^n - 5^n
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n, 5) + pow(3, n, 5) - pow(5, n, 5)) % 5 == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 10 divides n^5 - n
For all integers n, 10 divides n^5 - n
For all integers n, 30 divides n^5 - n
For all integers n, 60 divides n^5 - n when n is odd
For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 30 divides n^7 - n
For all integers n, 42 divides n^7 - n
For all integers n, 210 divides n^5 - n when n is divisible by 7
For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then n divides the sum of the first n-1 integers raised to the (n-2)nd power
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or (sum(pow(k, n-2, n) for k in range(1, n)) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n + 3^n ≡ 5 mod n
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n, n) + pow(3, n, n)) % n == 5 % n)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then 2^n + 3^n + 5^n ≡ 10 mod n
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n, n) + pow(3, n, n) + pow(5, n, n)) % n == 10 % n)

claim: For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then n^2 divides 2^n + 3^n - 5^n
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n, n*n) + pow(3, n, n*n) - pow(5, n, n*n)) % (n*n) == 0)

For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then n divides the sum of the first n-1 integers raised to the nth power
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or (sum(pow(k, n, n) for k in range(1, n)) % n == 0)

claim: For all integers n, 210 divides n^7 - n when n is divisible by 5
For all integers n, 2310 divides n^11 - n
For all integers n, 30030 divides n^13 - n
For all integers n > 1, if n is prime then n divides the product of the first n-1 integers raised to the (n-2)nd power
predicate: (n <= 1) or (not is_prime(n)) or (pow(math.prod(range(1, n)), n-2, n) % n == 0)
